I am super happy to able to share my daily routine as someone who is freshly new to the carnivore lifestyle! 🥰 All information shared, I’ve learned from @KenDBerryMD , who is a Board Certified Family Physician and the Proper Human Diet (PHD). He has 20 years of experience being a family physician and has treated thousands of patients using REAL, practical advice grounded in research and ancestral wisdom ❤️✨ #fyp #whatieatinaday #carnivore #carnivorediet #highproteinmeals

Hi friends happy Tuesday!! What are y’all’s goals for the week? Breakfast: protein oatmeal I did two packs of the chocolate chip banana bread protein oatmeal! I topped it off with fruit and almond butter. Lunch: loaded sweet potato I air fried my sweet potatoes for about 45 mins. Then I topped them with ground beef mixture, cottage cheese, avocado and hot honey. Ground beef: 93% lean meat, 2-3 tablespoons of siete taco seasoning, 2 tablespoons of tomato paste, 1/4 onion. dinner: ground beef quesadillas 3/4 cup of light cheese, 2 mission low carb flour tortillas, and my ground beef meal prep. I did 1/2 cup of good culture cottage and grilled some white onions with Serrano peppers. Ground beef: 93% lean meat, 2-3 tablespoons of siete taco seasoning, 2 tablespoons of tomato paste, 1/4 onion. #whatieatinaday #wieiad #eatwithme #mealideas #whatiatetoday #wieiadrealistic

Last year I was sure I was going to be out of recovery from surgery and able to make an appearance at the holiday work party, but let’s be honest. I had just had 80% of my tongue removed, had a tracheostomy, a feeding tube, and could barely talk to my partner and family. Flash forward to year later, and I sat down with a plate of risotto and prime rib and lobster and ate before I danced the night away! 10 months cancer free as of yesterday. 10 months that feel like a lifetime and no time at all. ❤️ Cheers yall- to health, happiness, and growth by any means necessary. caption: What if food could talk — and tell you the truth? Sugar turns your blood into sticky syrup and sends insulin through the roof. Instant noodles look harmless, but inside your gut they turn into glue and slow digestion to a crawl. Fried chicken tastes amazing, yet quietly coats your arteries with fat and puts stress on your heart. Red chili burns your tongue — and keeps burning deep in your stomach. But not all food is the enemy. Cucumber cools the fire and hydrates your body. Papaya helps break down junk food, cleanses your gut, and brings a natural glow to your skin. Garlic cuts through bad fat, clears blockages, and gets your blood moving. Red onion boosts iron levels and helps your body create fresh red blood cells. This video shows what different foods do inside your body — in a simple, visual, and slightly sarcastic way. Watch, learn, and maybe rethink what’s on your plate. #healthfacts #nutritiontips #guthealth #health #nutrition

Three years in and I finally feel like I know what I’m doing. Learn from my takeaways: 1. Buy a food scale and log everything you eat. 2. No hyper-restrictions, fad diets, fad exercises, fad “superfoods,” fad supplements, etc. 3. Don’t obsess over weight. Focus on how you look and feel. Weight is not the best metric for progress. 4. Intuition over metrics. Stop obsessing over what your fitness watch or app says. 5. Rest is not a reward, it is a necessity for progress. 6. Working out is a non-negotiable. Treat it like a part time job, except you get health currency instead of money. 7. Consistency is the key. Do everything with intention. 8. Saying no sometimes is a necessary sacrifice. Focus on your end goal, not your mood at that moment. #fitnesstips #fitnessgirlsmotivation #pcosweightloss #Fitness

God created natural foods for Us to eat The Bible doesn’t read like a nutrition book, but it quietly shows us something powerful - whole foods, natural fats, clean proteins, fruit, honey, herbs. No factories. No synthetic stacks. Just food from the earth. What people actually ate in the Bible📜 Bread made from wheat, barley, or spelt. �Figs, grapes, pomegranates, dates. �Olives and olive oil as the primary fat. �Lentils and beans for sustenance. �Milk and simple cheeses. �Honey as a rare sweetener. �Lamb, goat, beef, and birds. �Fish, especially in the New Testament. Wine is mentioned often as well. It was part of the historical and cultural context. That said, it is not something I believe should be consumed much. Still, it deserves its place here because this is about accuracy, not personal bias. What’s missing matters too. There’s no refined sugar. No industrial oils. No ultra processed food. No constant snacking. Meals were seasonal, local, and Shared. Eat biblically. #health #holistichealth #food

garlic & herb butter steak bites over mashed potatoes🥩🌿 recipe below❤️ garlic herb butter: -1/4 cup (1/2 a stick) softened butter -1/2 tbsp Dijon mustard -1/2 tbsp chives -1/2 tbsp parsley -2 tsp minced garlic -1 tsp lemon juice -1/4 tsp cayenne pepper, paprika, red pepper flakes, salt & pepper -makes 4 servings garlic herb butter steak bites: -trim 1lb sirloin steak tips into bites ~1inch -season with Worcestershire sauce, salt, pepper, & buttery steakhouse seasoning -sear in olive oil on medium/high heat (cast iron is best) -flip after 2 mins and sear -reduce heat and stir in 2 tbsp garlic herb butter in pan -remove steak and add asparagus (or vegetable of choice) in pan and sauté until fork tender (about 8 mins on medium) garlic bread: -slice baguette and brush with garlic herb butter -bake at 400 for 8-10 mins (or until golden brown & crispy) mashed potatoes: -peel & dice 2 russet potatoes -boil in salted water until fork tender -strain water & mash -stir in heavy cream (about 1/3 cup), salt, and pepper Serve: -Serve garlic herb butter steak bites over mashed potatoes. Top with butter and chives. CHIPOTLE BEEF STUFFED PEPPERS + HERBY RICE 🍋‍🟩🥑🫑 540 CALS 41G PROTEIN One of my fave high protein, low calorie meals. It’s actually super easy and so much fun to make! Perfect if you want to add something abit different to your weekly meal plan. Ingredients: 400g 3% fat beef (or kidney beans for a veggie alternative) 1 tin chopped tomatoes 2tbsp chipotle paste 1tbsp tomato puree 1tbsp paprika 1tbsp smoked paprika 1/2 onion 3 cloves garlic 1 red chilli 2 red peppers 2 handfuls mozzarella Long grain rice 1 lime Handful chopped corriander Optional garnish: 1tbsp Guacamole 1tbsp natural yog Method: Halve the red peppers, drizzle In olive oil and season. Bake in the oven for 25 minutes. Meanwhile, fry off the beef mince until brown. Then add onion, garlic and chilli. Cook for a few minutes. Add the tomato puree and chipotle paste and cook out for a few minutes. Then add the tinned toms and 1/2 tin water. Cook until reduced. Once the peppers are done, add the beef mince and mozzarella and bake for a further 20 minutes or until the cheese is golden brown. Serve over herby rice with a dollop of guacamole and yoghurt! Enjoy♥️ #easyrecipes #dinnerideas #healthyrecipes #highprotein #beefrecipe

caption: Garlic Parmesan chicken pasta ✨ Ingredients Chicken - 1.5 lbs chicken breast, cut in half - 1 tsp roasted garlic powder - 1 tsp smoked paprika - 1 tsp oregano - 1 tsp Italian seasoning - 1 tsp garlic and herb seasoning - Salt to taste - 1 tbsp avocado oil (for cooking) - 1 tbsp unsalted butter (for cooking) Sauce + pasta - 14 oz fettuccine pasta - 1 1/2 cup heavy cream - 6 tbsp unsalted butter - 1/4 cup pasta water - 7 garlic cloves (minced) - 1 shallot (minced) - 1 1/4 cups Parmesan cheese (grated) - 1 tbsp freshly chopped parsley - 1 1/2 tsp oregano - 1 1/2 tsp roasted garlic powder - 1 tsp red chili flakes - Salt and pepper to taste Instructions - In a large bowl coat chicken breast with about 2 tsp of olive oil, seasonings, salt and mix until evenly coated. Cover and set aside for 30 minutes to marinate. - Heat a skillet over medium-high heat. Add avocado oil and 1 tbsp of butter. - Sear chicken for 5 minutes per side until a golden crust forms. Deglaze pan with a little chicken broth, lower the heat, cover, and simmer until internal temperature reaches 155F. - Remove chicken from pan, cover in foil, and place into oven set warm. Pasta - Bring a large pot of water to a rolling boil. Salt it heavily. - Cook pasta according to package instructions until al dente. - Reserve at least 1/4 cup pasta water, then drain. Sauce - To the same pan (medium heat) add butter. - Sauté shallot for 1–2 minutes until soft. - Add minced garlic and cook until fragrant (about 30 seconds). - Season with oregano, garlic powder, chili flakes, salt, and black pepper. Mix until well combined and fragrant - Pour in heavy cream and mix - Simmer gently for 3–5 minutes until slightly thickened. - Lower heat and stir in Parmesan cheese and pasta water. Mix until well combined. Taste and adjust for seasonings. - Add cooked fettuccine and parsley, mixing until well coated. - Stir in an additional ¼ cup Parmesan until fully combined. - Finish with chopped parsley. - Add sliced chicken on top of pasta - Finish with extra Parmesan, cracked pepper, or chili flakes if desired. • • • #recipes #pasta #dinnerideas #chickenrecipe #foodideas

when you want something satisfying but still kinda healthy… this is it😮‍💨 Crispy chicken strips, homemade fries & a spicy greek yoghurt dip🤤✨ who’s making this for dinner this week?👀 For The Chicken: 2 Chicken breast (cut into strips) 1 egg Breadcrumbs Smoked Paprika Oregano Garlic powder Salt Spray oil Method: Add the chicken strips to a bowl, add 1tsp of each of the seasonings listed along with the egg, mix well then coat fully in the breadcrumb mix (season them with the same seasonings) Place in air fryer and lightly spray with oil.Air fry at 190–200°C until golden and crispy (around 12–15 minutes, depending on thickness). Flip halfway for even crispiness. Air Fryer Fries Ingredients: 2 large Potatoes Olive oil Oregano Salt Method: Cut potatoes into fries. Soak in water for 20–30 minutes (removes starch = crispier fries). Drain and pat completely dry. Drizzle with a small amount of oil. Season with oregano and salt. Air fry at 185°C for 15 minutes. Increase to 200°C for 5 minutes to crisp them up. For The Sauce Ingredients: Greek yogurt Sriracha Worcestershire sauce Salt Mix all ingredients together in a bowl Taste and adjust spice/salt level. #EasyRecipes #cookwithme #DinnerIdeas #cooking #easydinner

Honey Garlic Salmon bowls!🍣🍚 #cookinghacks #foodlover #homecooking #recipeinspo #foodie Ever wondered how viral chefs make mouthwatering meals? Here’s the recipe📝: Take skinless salmon filet, cut into cubes 1/3 cup soy sauce 1/4 cup honey 2 tsp sesame oil 4-5 cloves garlic, minced 1 tsp salt, 1 tsp pepper, 2 tsp onion 1 tsp paprika powder *let marinate for at least 10 minutes. Bake at 400°F for about 8-10 minutes, flipping and adding extra sauce halfway through. cucumber salad: 2 large cucumbers, sliced thinly - 1/4 cup soy sauce - 2 tbsp rice vinegar - 2 tsp sesame oil - 1 tbsp honey - 2 tbsp chili garlic crunch - 1 tsp salt, 1 tsp pepper, 2 tsp garlic powder, 2 tsp onion powder - 1 cup jasmine rice - 2 cups water - 1 tbsp rice vinegar - 1/2 tsp sugar top/ garnish with - green onion - furikake flakes - optional: siracha mayo

cajun chicken alfredo potatoes Ingredients: Two chicken breasts (butterflied and coated in 2 tsp cajun seasoning, 1 tsp garlic seasoning) 5 potatoes (cubed and coated in 2 tsp garlic seasoning, 2 tsp Italian seasoning) 1 onion 2 cloves garlic 1 tbsp butter 175g crème fraiche 80ml milk Salt & pepper 1 tsp Italian seasoning 30g Parmesan - add the prepped potatoes to the airfryer, coat in the seasoning and a drizzle of oil and airfry for 30 minutes on 180. - add the chicken to an oiled pan and cook for 15 minutes until white through. - add the chopped onion to a pan, brown and then add in the garlic. Turn the heat down and add in the butter, leave this to melt, then add in half the crème fraiche and milk. Stir this until combined then add the other half of crème fraiche and stir again. Add the seasoning and Parmesan, then leave on a low simmer for 5 minutes - once the chicken is cooked, chop into chunks and add this to the sauce. - layer this mixture onto the potatoes, and garnish with parsley #mealinspo #dinnerideas #mealideas #chickenrecipes

Prepare the salad base by combining chopped cucumber, lettuce, tomato and red onion in a bowl. 6. Assemble by adding the crispy pasta to the salad and tossing everything together with the dressing. #EasyRecipes #pastasalad #lunchideas #healthyrecipe #cookwithme

Street Corn Chicken Rice Bowls 🤤Chicken, creamy street corn & cilantro lime rice topped w/a smoky dressing. Balanced & easy to prep ahead for a busy weeknight 👏🏼 Recipe below ⬇️ or grab it at https://mallorythedietitian.com/street-corn-chicken-rice-bowl/ ….ooorrrr search on Google for “mallorythedietitian street corn chicken rice bowl” INGREDIENTS For Chicken • 1½ lb chicken breasts (or thighs) • 3 Tbsp extra virgin olive oil (1 for marinading + 2 for cooking) • 1½ tsp chili powder • 1 tsp each: smoked paprika, garlic powder • ½ tsp onion powder • ½ tsp kosher salt • ¼ tsp black pepper For Dressing • 1 c Greek yogurt • 2 Tbsp mayo • 1 lime, zest & juice • ½ tsp each: chili powder, smoked paprika, garlic powder • ½ tsp kosher salt • ¼ tsp black pepper • 1-3 Tbsp water (as needed to thin it out) For Street Corn • 3 c corn, cooked • ½ red bell pepper, chopped • 1 small jalapeno, chopped • ¼ c red onion, chopped • ¼ c cilantro, chopped • ¼ c cotija cheese For Cilantro Lime Rice • 1 c jasmine rice, uncooked • 1 Tbsp extra virgin olive oil • ½ c cilantro, chopped • 2 Tbsp lime juice • ½ tsp each: garlic powder, kosher salt • ¼ tsp black pepper For Assembling (opt) • romaine • avo • tomatoes DIRECTIONS 1. Prep chicken: Cut chicken into cubes. Coat in 1 Tbsp olive oil + seasonings. Set aside to let marinade. 2. Make rice: cook rice according to package. Mix in cilantro, lime, olive oil, gp, s&p. 3. Prep Dressing: make the Elote dressing by mixing the ingredients listed. 4. Make Street Corn: remove corn from cob & chop the jalapeno, red pepper, onion, & cilantro. Add everything to a bowl. Top w/cotija cheese & half the dressing. 5. Cook chicken: heat a skillet on medium-high. Add olive oil until hot. Add chicken & cook 3-4 min on one side. Flip & cook another 3-4 min until cooked to internal temp 165℉ (75°C) 6. Assemble: Add romaine (opt), rice, chicken & corn to a bowl. Top with more dressing, lime, & fave toppings! NUTRITION (per bowl, makes 6)* 470 cal, 34g pro, 43g carb, 19g fat *nutrition info based on serving size rec: ½ c chicken, ½ c rice, ¾ c corn salad, 2 Tbsp dressing, ½ c romaine (opt) Follow for healthy recipes! caption: One-Pan Chicken Shawarma Rice Bowls 🔥 All the shawarma flavor, none of the extra steps… juicy spiced chicken, onion rice pilaf, refreshing salad and a creamy garlic sauce 🤌 For the Chicken: -1.5 lbs (680g) boneless, skinless chicken thighs -Seasoning: 1 tbsp paprika, 1 tbsp cumin, 1 tbsp onion powder, 2 tsp coriander, 1 tsp allspice, 2 tsp turmeric, 2 tsp pepper, 1 tsp salt -3 tbsp olive oil + 8 garlic cloves -½ lemon, juiced -2 tsp bouillon (I used the roasted chicken base from @Better Than Bouillon) For the Rice: -3 tbsp butter + 1 ½ cups yellow onion, diced -1 tbsp turmeric, 2 tsp paprika, 2 tsp garlic powder, 2 tsp pepper -1 ½ cups basmati rice -3 cups hot water -3 tsp bouillon For the Fresh Sumac Salad: -½ large red onion, thinly sliced -1 cup cherry tomatoes, quartered -1 cup cucumber, sliced then halved -1 tsp salt, 1 tsp black pepper & 2 tsp sumac -½ lemon, juiced For the Garlic Sauce: -¼ cup mayonnaise + ¼ cup plain Greek yogurt -2 to 3 garlic cloves + 1 tbsp fresh parsley -1 tsp salt + 1 tsp black pepper -½ lemon, juiced + 1 tbsp olive oil 1. In a bowl, combine the chicken with the spices, olive oil, garlic, lemon juice and bouillon. Mix well. Marinate for as long as your patience can handle. 2. Heat a large pan over medium-high heat and add a drizzle of olive oil. Once hot, add the chicken in batches. Cook for 6 to 8 mins per side until golden and cooked through. Set aside. 3. Reduce heat to medium-low. Add butter to the same pan then the onions & cook, stirring often, until translucent. Add the garlic & spices, cook for 1 to 2 mins. Add the rice and toast for 2 mins while stirring. Pour in the hot water mixed with bouillon according to package instructions. Stir & bring to a boil. Once boiling, cover & reduce heat to low. Simmer for 20 mins until cooked. 4. Mix the salad ingredients in one bowl and the sauce ingredients in another bowl. Enjoy! #dinner #easyrecipe #homecooking #tiktokrecipe #fyp #shawarma #chickenrecipe #cookingathometiktoktv

caption: I lost 20 lbs with this meal. Try these easy, healthy, and delicious steamed veggie & meat rolls. Steamed Napa cabbage rolls 🧡 Ingredients (1 serving) 10 napa cabbage leaves 150g thinly sliced beef 10 perilla leaves 1 pack enoki mushrooms 1 pack mung bean sprouts -For the dipping sauce 2 tbsp soy sauce 1 tbsp allulose (or sugar/honey) 1 tbsp vinegar 1 tbsp minced garlic 1 stalk green onion Optional: a small amount of Korean mustard (yeon-gyoja) for an extra kick 💙 Directions 1. Separate the napa cabbage leaves and blanch them in boiling water until soft. 2. On each blanched cabbage leaf, place a perilla leaf, a slice of beef, and a few enoki mushrooms. Roll tightly. 3. Line a steamer with bean sprouts and place the cabbage rolls on top. Steam for 10 minutes. 4. While steaming, mix all the sauce ingredients together. 5. Cabbage Rolls 🥬#sugarfree #lowcarb #prediabetes Healthy and delicious cabbage rolls that are sugar-free and super low in carbs. Perfect for a complete dinner that keeps my husband's glucose stable and leaves us feeling super satisfied. INGREDIENTS: 🥬 Filling: 8 oz white button mushrooms, diced 1 lb ground chicken 3 green onions (white parts chopped for the filling, green parts for garnish) 4 large garlic cloves, minced 1 tbsp low-sodium soy sauce (or coconut aminos) ⅛ tsp sesame oil ⅛ tsp ginger powder 1 tsp salt (or to taste) Pinch of black pepper Avocado oil for cooking 🥢 Sauce: 2 tbsp low-sodium soy sauce 1 tbsp allulose 1 tsp rice vinegar ¼ tsp sesame oil ⅛ tsp garlic powder 1 tsp green onions (green parts), diced Pinch of black pepper ✨ For Assembly & Serving: 1 large Napa cabbage Sesame seeds and the green parts of the green onions for garnish Instructions: 1. FILLING: Heat a drizzle of avocado oil in a skillet. Add the diced mushrooms with a little salt and cook until they have released all their water. Add the white parts of the green onions and the garlic, and cook for another minute. Season with pepper, ginger powder, and soy sauce. Add the ground chicken and cook until it is well done. Remove from the heat and stir in the sesame oil. 2. CABBAGE: While the filling is cooking, boil the cabbage leaves in water for about 5-10 minutes, or until the white part is tender and bendy. Carefully remove them and pat them very dry with a paper towel. 3. ASSEMBLE & COOK: Place about 1 ½ tablespoons of filling on each cabbage leaf and roll it up. In a clean skillet over medium-high heat, pan-fry the rolls on all sides until golden brown. 4. SAUCE & SERVING: In a small bowl, mix all the sauce ingredients. Serve the rolls hot, pour the sauce over them, and garnish with the green parts of the green onions and sesame seeds.

If I had to redo my 40 kilo weight loss in five months, this is what I’d eat instead of protein oats. If you’re just starting out, you’ve honestly lucked out because this is pretty much the perfect breakfast to build your journey around. Each portion is around 300 calories with about 30 grams of protein, very low in carbs, and full of veggies. That combo keeps you full, keeps cravings quiet, and helps your blood sugar stay steady so you are not crashing or constantly thinking about food. Protein slows digestion, the fats help with satiety, and the fibre from the vegetables supports insulin sensitivity. Put together, it is exactly what you want first thing in the morning if fat loss is the goal. When you reheat it, you might notice a bit of water separating. That happens because of the eggs, vegetables, and cottage cheese. I personally do not mind it at all. It feels like a warm, savoury, slightly soupy start to the day. If you prefer it more like a firm baked slice, you can play around with it. Add a little oats for structure, remove the cottage cheese from the bake, and then add cottage cheese fresh on the side instead. That is the beauty of meals like this. You can adjust them endlessly based on your goals and your taste. If you are at the beginning of your weight loss journey, this kind of breakfast makes consistency easy. And consistency is what actually gets you results.

Your diet isn’t failing… your structure is 👇 Eating at 6am, 12pm and 7pm sounds “clean” But it often leads to this: → snacking all day → low energy → overeating at night Here’s why: 1️⃣ Meals too far apart → hunger builds up → bad decisions 2️⃣ Not enough protein → you’re never really full 3️⃣ Snacking between meals → constant hunger cycle 👉 Fix this instead: Eat balanced meals. Keep them closer together. Stop opening new eating windows all day. If you want something sweet → have it after your meal. 👉 Fat loss gets easier when hunger is controlled. Not when you rely on discipline. 📘 Comment “GUIDE” for my free fat-loss guide 📩 DM “ELITE” for 1:1 coaching 👊 Follow @luc_asthetic_ for fat loss advice

caption: didn’t think the drink through 🫢 #dadtok #fyp #foodreview #mealprep #barbecue today's lunch for dad: mac 'n cheese, bbq ribs, tomato caesar salad Bbq ribs ⬇️ 1) Season the ribs with a pinch of salt and soy sauce. 2) Pour 2 cups of water into a pressure cooker. 3) Place a rack inside the pressure cooker. 4) Lay the ribs on the rack. 5) Cover with the lid and cook on high pressure for 30 minutes. 6) Remove the ribs and coat them with your favorite barbecue sauce. 7) Bake in an air fryer at 350°F for another 7-10 minutes. Serve. Mac 'n cheese ⬇️ 1) Bring a pot of water to a boil. Add 8oz of shell pasta and cook for 5-6 minutes. Do not overcook, pasta should be slightly underdone. 2) Drain the pasta using a sieve and set aside. 3) In another pot, add 8 oz milk, 1½ tablespoons unsalted butter, 1 cup shredded mozzarella cheese, ½ cup shredded parmesan cheese. 4) Cook over medium heat, stirring continuously, until the butter and cheese are fully melted and the sauce is smooth. 5) Add the partially cooked pasta to the cheese sauce. Stir until the pasta is evenly coated 6) Add a pinch of salt, then cook briefly until the pasta reaches your desired tenderness. Serve.

caption: These meatballs were insanely good and packed with protein 👩🏻‍🍳 Turkey meatball recipe: 1. Preheat oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit. In a large mixing bowl, combine one pound ground turkey, 1 egg, 1/2 cup bread crumbs, 1/2 cup grated parmesan, 1 tbsp paprika, 1 tbsp italian seasoning, 1 tablespoon onion powder, 1 tablespoon garlic powder, 1 teaspoon salt, 1 teaspoon red chili flakes. Drizzle in about a tablespoon of olive oil, 1/4 cup chopped parsley, and mix together everything with your hands until everything is combined fully. 2. Line a large baking sheet. Grab the ground beef mixture in about increments of 2 tablespoons and roll balls with your hands. place on baking sheet. 3. Put those in the oven for about 25 minutes at 400°F. 4. As those are baking, start to make your sauce. In a small bowl mixed together a half cup soy sauce or coconut amino and 1/4 cup honey, and 1 tsp red chili flakes. mixed together and then place it all in a saucepan over medium high heat until the sauce thickens. 5. once the meatballs are done place them in the sauce and make sure they are all fully coated. #dinnerideas #easydinner #dinnerinspo #quickandeasyrecipe #weeknightdinner #baking #healthydinner

If you think “healthy” food has to be bland or boring, let me change your mind with this one! For me, I’ve noticed I feel my best eating a higher-protein, lower-carb diet, so I love satisfying recipes like this that make me feel good during and after eating😊 Recipe below + full recipe details in biooooo as always :) #RECIPE Low-Carb Chicken “Dumplings” (makes ~12) 🥬 INGREDIENTS - 1 lb ground chicken - 1 head napa cabbage (leaves separated), I used 12 leaves - 4 green onions, finely chopped - 3 garlic cloves, minced - 2 tsp fresh ginger, minced - 3 tbsp soy sauce (or tamari for gluten-free) - 1 tbsp sesame oil - Salt + black pepper to taste - Optional: 1–2 tsp gochugaru (Korean red pepper flakes) 👩🏻‍🍳 INSTRUCTIONS Bring a large pot of water to a boil. Separate napa cabbage leaves and blanch for 1–2 minutes until pliable (do longer if using green cabbage). Pat dry or let drain and set aside. In a large bowl, combine ground chicken, garlic, ginger, green onions, soy sauce, sesame oil, salt, pepper, and optional gochugaru. Mix well until fully combined. Lay a cabbage leaf flat. Add a spoonful of filling near the thicker white stem. Roll just enough to cover the filling, fold the sides inward, then continue rolling tightly like a burrito to fully seal. Place finished dumplings seam-side down. Cook using one of the methods below: - Steam: Steam dumplings seam-side down for 15 minutes, until fully cooked. - Pan-fry: Cook seam-side down in neutral oil over medium heat for 4–5 minutes. Flip and cook another 4–5 minutes. Increase heat to medium-high and cook 1–2 minutes more, turning to crisp all sides. Serve warm with your favorite dipping sauce. I love topping mine with chili oil. #highprotein #lowcarb #healthyrecipe #easyrecipe

Korean weight-loss recipe series EP.3 Cabbage Rice Bowl 🧡 Ingredients 200g cabbage 1/2 onion 1/2 green onion 1 tbsp minced garlic 1 egg 1 tbsp soy sauce 1/3 tsp sugar 1 tbsp oyster sauce 1 tsp sesame oil Sesame seeds for garnish 💙 Directions 1. Thinly slice the green onion, onion, and cabbage. 2. Heat some olive oil in a pan over low heat, then sauté the green onion and minced garlic until fragrant. 3. Add the onion, cabbage, soy sauce, sugar, and oyster sauce, then stir-fry over high heat. 4. Continue cooking until the vegetables release some moisture and it becomes slightly saucy, then crack an egg in the center and cook it to your liking. 5. Serve the cabbage stir-fry over white rice, brown rice, or tofu, according to your preference. 🔖 Save this recipe for your next meal 🥰 Like, follow & comment if you enjoyed . . . . . . #koreanfood #easyrecipes #healthyrecipe #mukbang #weightloss

Save my recipe for a quick and easy meal that the whole family will love! ➡️ Recipe: Chicken pan fried noodles 🍜 2 cups cabbage, thinly sliced 4-5 stalks of green onions, separated white parts from green. Slice white parts in half and green parts cut into 3 in pieces. 1 medium onion, thinly sliced 1 medium carrot, peeled and cut into matchstick strips 4-5 garlic cloves, finely minced 1 lb fresh wheat noodles, par cooked (see note below) can also substitute for egg noodles or cooked spaghetti if needed. Cooking oil 1 lb Chicken thighs, 1/4 inch slices 2 tbsp Oyster sauce 1/2 tbsp Soy sauce 1/4 tsp Pepper 1/4 tsp Baking soda Sauce— 1 1/2 tbsp Soy sauce 2 tbsp Dark soy sauce 2 tbsp Oyster sauce 1 tbsp Shaoxing wine 1/2 tbsp Sugar 2 tsp sesame oil 1. Par boil your fresh wheat noodles for 1 minute or cook until al dente for other noodle options. Strain and set aside. 2. Marinate sliced chicken thighs with oyster sauce, soy sauce, pepper and baking soda. Mix well and marinade for 15 mins. 3. In small bowl, combine soy sauce, dark soy sauce, oyster sauce, shaoxing wine and sugar. Mix well and set aside. 4. In a large wok heat 2 tbsp of cooking oil on high heat. Sauté marinaded chicken until cooked through. Remove from pan. 5. Add 2 more tbsp of cooking oil to the wok then sauté white parts of green onions until fragrant. Add garlic and onions and sauté until onions are slightly translucent. 6. Add cabbage and carrots then sauté for 1-2 minutes. Add the par cooked noodles, cooked chicken and pour the sauce over everything. Toss everything together. Cook until the noodles absorb all the sauce about 3-4 mins. 7. Finish it off with a drizzle of sesame oil and the green parts of the the green onions. Give it one last toss. Serve and enjoy! #panfriednoodles #noodles #takeout #chickenrecipe #easyrecipe #mealideas #recipe

Garlic Parmesan chicken skewers, crispy baby potatoes & a side salad… simple, High protein, and perfect for summer time evenings✨🥗 🧄 Full Recipe👇🏼 Chicken skewers 2 chicken breasts, diced 1 tbsp Greek yogurt 1 tsp smoked paprika ½ tsp garlic powder ½ tsp dried oregano Salt & pepper Garlic butter topping 1 tbsp butter, melted 1 garlic clove, minced Small handful fresh coriander, chopped 2 tbsp grated parmesan Crispy potatoes 300–400g baby potatoes, halved ½ tsp dried oregano Salt & pepper Simple salad Lettuce Cucumber Tomato Red onion Instructions 1. Prep the chicken: In a bowl mix the diced chicken with Greek yogurt, smoked paprika, garlic powder, oregano, salt and pepper. Thread onto skewers. 2. Bake the chicken: Bake at 200°C (fan 180°C) for 18–20 minutes, until cooked through. 3. Make the garlic butter: Mix melted butter with minced garlic, chopped coriander and parmesan. 4. Finish the skewers: Brush the garlic butter mixture over the cooked chicken skewers. 5. Crispy potatoes: Toss halved baby potatoes with oregano, salt and pepper. Bake at 200°C for 30–35 minutes until golden and crispy. 6. Salad: Chop lettuce, cucumber, tomato and red onion and serve on the side. #EasyRecipes #cookwithme #DinnerIdeas #healthyrecipes #highprotein

Here's my take 👇🏻 Great for busy nights, batch lunches or when your brain just isn't computing. What I used (serves 4): 200g rice (I used brown) 450g chicken breast, chopped 70g cheddar 50g parmesan 50g green pesto (this can be subbed for red pesto, harissa or tikka paste) 200g Brussels sprouts, or broccoli, or cauliflower 300g cherry tomatoes 400ml bone broth Salt and pepper to taste Thanks to @adamhoad_coaching for these accessible meals ideas - check out his page. So so great 💚 Method: 1. Rinse the rice 2. Combine everything except the cheese in an ovenproof dish. Mix well 3. Top with cheddar and parmesan 4. Cover with foil and bake at 190°C for 50-55 minutes 5. Uncover for the final 5 minutes for a crispy cheese finish 6. Leave the dish sit for 20 mins to cool slightly and so the rice absorbs the broth Approx nutrition per serving (with bone broth): 490 kcal 45–50g protein (incredible) 38g carbs 18g fat 8g fibre Using bone broth instead of stock adds a bonus protein boost, plus collagen and gut-supportive amino acids - without changing the flavour or effort. I use a powder which is more cost effective and a really great cupboard staple. Definitely try this so so easy and versatile ✨ #nutritionistapproved #viralrecipe #onepandinner #highprotein #collagen #bonebroth #easydinner

What’s the first thing you would make if you had this air fryer? 👀 This week I put together a healthier version of green Mexican spaghetti with shrimp — and honestly, I barely had to change anything to lighten it up. It’s high in protein, super satiating, and really easy to meal prep. Each portion comes in at 531 calories, 73g of protein, and 5g of fiber. If you want to make this exact version, the full recipe process is already in my cookbook — you’ll just be combining two recipes that are already there: 🔸 Season your shrimp the same way we season the shrimp tacos on pages 10–11 🔸 Make the green spaghetti the same way we make it for the chicken version on pages 34–35 Put those two together and you get this high-protein, flavorful meal prep with the exact macros listed above. Btw the air fryer im using is an @Gourmia french door air fryer toster and oven! They have a black friday sale on it at Wal mart so go check it out! Let me know if you make it! 💚🍤🍝

Part 7 🌎🍽️ Patatas Bravas from 🇪🇸SPAIN🇪🇸 8.2/10!! Cam served his mission in Spain, and loved this dish while he was there! For sure gonna make it again. Ingredients: * 10 gold potatoes * lots of olive oil * 6 cloves garlic * 1/2 a yellow onion * 1 tbsp smoked paprika * 3/4 tsp salt * 1/4 tsp chili flakes * 1/4 tsp pepper * 3 tomatoes * 3/4 cup chicken broth * 1/3 cup mayonnaise * 2 tbsp lemon juice * salt and pepper Preheat your oven to 425°F. Start by boiling some water in a pot with some salt. Wash your gold potatoes and cut them into cubes. Boil until fork tender. Drain the potatoes and add them to a large bowl. Put in a good amount of olive oil (roughly between 3 tbsp-1/4 cup) and give them a good stir to rough them up a bit. The rough exterior makes them more crispy. Add the potatoes to a baking sheet and drizzle some more olive oil on top, along with some salt and pepper. Also add 3 garlic cloves on the sheet (still in their peel). You’ll add that to the aioli later. Bake between 30-45 minutes or until desired crispiness. You can also start broiling at the end to speed up the process. To make the red sauce, sauté the onion and 3 cloves garlic in some olive oil for roughly 8 minutes. Then add the smoked paprika, salt, pepper, and chili flakes. After a minute or 2 add the tomatoes. Sauté for another 5 minutes for adding the chicken broth. Let that simmer together for 5-10 minutes before adding to a blender. For the garlic aioli, blend together the mayonnaise with the roasted garlic (take off the peel), lemon juice, and salt and pepper. Assemble the patatas bravas and you’re done! #easyrecipes #patatasbravas #spain #cookwithme #foodie #dinnerideas #internationalfood #potatoes #cozyrecipes
